items.title: Wall Street Rebounds As Cooling Inflation Draws Tech Dip Buyers: What's Driving Markets Wednesday?
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Wall Street rebounded as cooling inflation data fueled expectations of Fed rate cuts, driving a rally in tech stocks led by Tesla, Nvidia, and Palantir.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company issued a negative outlook for the quarter, and Bank of America lowered its stock price target.
The stock dropped after major banks lowered their price targets.
The stock surged after a report revealed that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company had approached Nvidia, AMD, and Broadcom about investing in a joint venture to operate Intel's factories.
The stock rallied as part of the tech-led rebound.
The stock rallied as part of the tech-led rebound.
The stock rallied as part of the tech-led rebound.
The Dow remained flat despite the overall market rebound.
The Nasdaq 100 index, which the QQQ ETF tracks, gained 1.2% as part of the tech-led rebound.
The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund outperformed, gaining 1.8%.

items.title: Inflation Cools More Than Expected In February, Strengthens The Case For Fed Rate Cuts
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Inflation eased in February, coming in lower than expected and reinforcing the Federal Reserve's path toward interest rate cuts. The headline Consumer Price Index fell to 2.8% year-over-year, and core inflation slowed to 3.1%, strengthening the case for Fed rate cuts in 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The softer-than-expected inflation data and the potential for Fed rate cuts are positive for the stock market, as evidenced by the rise in S&P 500 futures.
